_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED ("DO NOT EDIT") files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. Provenance: issue #88 (reporter @AndrewITLive), fixed 2026-06-15 on press 4.24.0.

handfixes.summary: Sync loop fetches all pages for NinjaOne after-id (keyset) endpoints. NinjaOne list endpoints (/v2/devices, /v2/organizations, ...) return a bare JSON array and paginate via ?after=<lastEntityId> - no body cursor, no envelope has_more. The generated loop only handled envelope-cursor / page / offset paginators, so it stopped at the first full page and reported a truncated dataset (e.g. 1000 of 1115 devices) as complete. --max-pages 0 had no effect because the loop broke on !hasMore before the cap mattered.
handfixes.why: determinePaginationDefaults() hard-codes cursorParam/cursorType "cursor", and extractPageItems() returns hasMore=false for a bare array (sync.go), so the natural-end break (`if !hasMore || len(items) < pageSize.limit`) fired after page 1. The press's pagination profiler does not model after-id/keyset cursors; a plain reprint regenerates the same defaults (the 0.1.1 4.24.0 regen produced byte-identical code, confirming this). The fix adds a response-shape-driven after-id fallback: when a bare-array page is full and the last item exposes an id, advance under ?after=<lastId>. Object-enveloped responses (/v2/queries/* carry a `cursor` object) are unaffected. Applied to BOTH the flat syncResource loop and the syncDependentResource loop.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: DURABLE FIX belongs in cli-printing-press: teach the pagination profiler to detect after-id/keyset pagination (bare-array list + ?after param + id-derived cursor) and emit it natively. File via printing-press-retro. Until the press learns it, this hand-fix must survive every reprint.
